rule.name: 'also' at the end of the sentence
rule.message: 'Also' is not used at the end of the sentence. Use as well or too instead.

rule.name: Word order: 'Hopefully' starting a sentence
rule.message: Starting a sentence with 'hopefully' may be considered bad style.

rule.name: and also (and/also)
rule.message: Consider using and or also.

rule.name: are able to (can)
rule.message: This expression is wordy. Consider using can.

rule.name: best ever (best)
rule.message: This phrase might be redundant. Consider using \1.

rule.name: in order to (to)
rule.message: This phrase might be considered redundant. Consider using \3.

rule.name: blend together (blend)
rule.message: This phrase might be redundant. Consider using \1.

rule.name: brief moment/summary (moment/summary)
rule.message: Consider using \2.

rule.name: AppStore (App Store, Amazon Appstore)
rule.message: If you mean the Apple digital distribution platform, use App Store. For Amazon, use Amazon Appstore.

rule.name: a la (à la)
rule.message: ‘A la’ is a foreign expression which originally has a diacritic. à la

rule.name: require typewriter (straight) apostrophe (')
rule.message: Consider using the typewriter apostrophe. \1

rule.name: require typographical (curly) apostrophe (’)
rule.message: Consider using the typographical apostrophe. \1

rule.name: can not (cannot)
rule.message: Did you mean cannot?

rule.name: flashpoint (flash point)
rule.message: Did you mean flash point?

rule.name: comparison 'as ... as' (no comparative/superlative)
rule.message: Did you mean \2?

rule.name: He has much (a lot of) money
rule.message: In this context, a lot of or so much is more likely.

rule.name: They's (They'd) already read his biography
rule.message: Typo detected: Did you mean to write '\1'd' (contracted form of '\1 had' or '\1 would')? \1'd

rule.name: Collocation: Popular among/with
rule.message: The usual collocation for "popular" is "with", not "among". popular with

rule.name: Collocation: guard from/against
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "against", not "\2". Did you mean \1 against?

rule.name: Collocation: in front of
rule.message: The usual preposition for "in front" is "of". Did you mean in front of?

rule.name: Collocation: in/on an album
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\3" is "on", not "in" when "album" refers to a collection of recorded music. If by "album" you mean a collection of photos, "in an album" is correct. Did you mean on \2 \3?

rule.name: Collocation: passionate by/about (stricter)
rule.message: The usual preposition for "passionate" is "about", not "by". \1 passionate about

rule.name: waiting (for) a patient
rule.message: The word "for" is missing between "\1" and "\1 \2". \1 for \2 \3

rule.name: Creative Writing: E-Prime: 'to be' as the main verb
rule.message: According to E-Prime, avoiding the use of the stative verb 'to be' makes the communication clearer, more accurate, and less dogmatic. Use an alternative verb.
rule.short: Using E-Prime, you should avoid the verb 'to be'.

rule.name: Creative Writing: E-Prime: all 'to be' forms
rule.message: According to E-Prime, avoiding the use of the stative verb 'to be' makes the communication clearer, more accurate, and less dogmatic. Use an alternative verb.
rule.short: Using E-Prime, you should avoid the verb 'to be'.

rule.name: It is obvious (unscientific)
rule.message: If possible, be specific about why that is obvious.

rule.name: really, very, quite, extremely, severely (use sparingly)
rule.message: Usually, the word "\1" is not necessary. Remove it unless it is necessary.
